analysis_result: For CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP (MSigDB C6) in MPNST-G2 (vs MPNST-G1): NES = +1.31 (positively enriched in G2), FDR q-value = 0.344. Across alternative ranking metrics, NES ranged 1.26-1.32 and FDR 0.20-0.34. Direction confirms upregulation of the CYCLIN_D1_UP signature in MPNST-G2 (more aggressive) vs MPNST-G1, though the FDR did not reach the conventional 0.25 significance threshold in this reanalysis.
denominator_used: n = 12 MPNST tumors with RNA-seq (6 MPNST-G1: M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803; 6 MPNST-G2: M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413). One G2 sample (M1933, RNA label RT1933) was excluded because its RNA label was not found in the GSE207400 expression matrix.
assumptions_made: Used gseapy.prerank (v1.1.13) on GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz (log-normalized bulk RNA-seq, 14992 unique genes after dropping duplicates and NaN); ranked genes by signal-to-noise ratio (G2 vs G1) with 1000 gene-set permutations and seed 42; gene-set library = MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures (C6, 187 sets passed min_size=15/max_size=500 filter); CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP gene set contains 188 genes; G2 defined as the positive phenotype so positive NES = up in G2; Tumor_ID was used as the expression-matrix column key since all G1 samples and 6/7 G2 samples matched by Tumor_ID; M1933/RT1933 dropped as absent from matrix.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz: log-normalized bulk RNA-seq, 14995 rows x 48 samples (14992 unique genes after dedup/NaN removal).
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx: clinical table, 108 rows, columns include Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, Methylation_Group, RNA-Seq, RNA label.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter: Tumor Type == 'MPNST' AND RNA-Seq == 'X' -> 13 MPNST with RNA-seq.
   - Further filter: Methylation_Group in {MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2} and Tumor_ID present as a column in the expression matrix -> 12 samples (6 G1, 6 G2). M1933 (G2) excluded because its RNA label 'RT1933' was not found in the GSE207400 matrix.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Phenotype class vector: 6 x MPNST_G1 (negative/reference), 6 x MPNST_G2 (positive/phenotype).
   - G2 samples: M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413.
   - G1 samples: M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803.
   - Rank metric: signal-to-noise ratio (mean_G2 - mean_G1) / (sd_G2 + sd_G1) per gene, with zero-protection epsilon.

D. COMPUTATION
   - gseapy.prerank with MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures (C6); permutation_num=1000, seed=42, min_size=15, max_size=500, 187 sets tested.
   - Gene set of interest: 'CYCLIN D1 UP.V1 UP' (188 genes).
   - Sensitivity analyses repeated with: (i) bounded signal-to-noise (Broad GSEA formula), (ii) raw mean-difference, (iii) Welch t-statistic.

E. RESULT
   - CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP, MPNST-G2 vs MPNST-G1:
     * Primary (S2N prerank): NES = +1.314, ES = 0.300, NOM p = 0.047, FDR q = 0.344.
     * Bounded S2N prerank:  NES = +1.258, FDR q = 0.267.
     * Mean-diff prerank:    NES = +1.256, FDR q = 0.202.
     * Welch-t prerank:      NES = +1.319, FDR q = 0.317.
   - Direction: positive NES -> pathway upregulated in MPNST-G2 (aggressive) vs MPNST-G1, consistent with the paper's Supplementary Figure 6b concept of CYCLIN_D1_UP enrichment in G2.

F. CAVEATS
   - Small sample size (6 vs 6) yields wide null distribution; FDR is larger than typical published GSEA run with 1000+ permutations in larger cohorts.
   - The paper likely used classic Broad GSEA (Java) or fgsea with different permutation type (phenotype vs gene-set) or different rank metric; exact FDR may differ but direction and magnitude of NES match a G2-enriched signature.
   - One G2 sample excluded because M1933/RT1933 RNA label not in expression matrix - this reduces power vs paper's 7-G2 cohort if they included it.
   - Log-normalized expression assumed; no further filtering of low-expression genes.
